response:
In C# it is possible to measure the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it requires some additional steps  
UDP does not guarantee delivery or timing information  
so you need to implement your own timing mechanism  

You can use the Stopwatch class to track the time before sending the packet  
and again after receiving a response  
this will give you the round trip time  

However if you only want one way timing  
you need to send a packet and wait for a response  
this is not straightforward with UDP  

You can use the Socket class to send a UDP packet  
and then wait for a response using the Receive method  
this will allow you to calculate the time taken for the packet to reach the server  

Keep in mind that UDP does not provide built in timing features  
so you need to handle this manually  

By using the Stopwatch class and the Socket class  
you can create a simple one way timing mechanism for UDP packets
